channel.title: Latest Finextra Research Artificial intelligence Headlines
channel.description: The Latest Financial IT Artificial intelligence Headlines

item.title: AI-powered payment disputes platform Casap raises $25m
item.description: Casap, a firm using AI to automate payment disputes and tackle first party fraud, has raised $25 million in a Series A funding round led by Emergence Capital.

item.title: Maybank signs RM1bn digital transformation deal with Microsoft
item.description: Malaysia's largest financial institution Maybank, has agreed a deal with Microsoft that will see the tech firm spearhead the bank's digital transformation

item.title: Wells Fargo enlists Google Cloud for agentic AI play
item.description: Wells Fargo is expanding its relationship with Google Cloud to roll out agentic AI across its operations.

item.title: Alaan raises $48m for spend management platform
item.description: Alaan, a UAE-based, AI-powered corporate spend management platform founded by former McKinsey consultants, has raised $48 million in Series A funding.

item.title: NatWest and Google to boost UK startups with AI tools and training
item.description: NatWest is working with Google Cloud to support entrepreneurs on the UK lender's business Accelerator with access to AI tools, cloud technology, and expert training.

item.title: SEC launches AI task force
item.description: The US Securities and Exchange Commission has launched an AI task force to spearhead the agency’s efforts to embed artificial intelligence into its operations.

item.title: AI-powered data platform Daloopa raises $13m
item.description: Daloopa, a provider of AI-powered data extraction services to hedge funds, investment banks, and other financial institutions, has raised $13 million in funding.

item.title: EBA warns of money laundering risk in fintech sector
item.description: The ongoing digitalisation of financial services has increased the risk of money laundering (ML) and terrorist financing (TF), according to the European Banking Authority (EBA).

item.title: Atom Bank restructures tech leadership team
item.description: UK-based Atom Bank has announced a series of changes to its technology senior leadership team, including a chief of staff and a new AI and data science team.

item.title: RBC taps proprietary AI model for credit decisions
item.description: Royal Bank of Canada is using its own proprietary AI foundation model to improve credit adjudication capabilities and loyalty programme personalisation.

item.title: Ramp closes second raise in 45 days as AI fuels growth
item.description: Less than two months after raising $200 million at a $16 billion valuation, Ramp has pulled in another $500 million in a round that values the expense management startup at $22.5 billion.

item.title: Standard Chartered signs AI deal with Alibaba
item.description: Standard Chartered has signed an AI and cloud deal with Albibaba Group.

item.title: Broadridge invests in AI platform Uptiq
item.description: Broadridge Financial Solutions, Inc. (NYSE: BR), a global Fintech leader, has announced a new strategic partnership and minority investment in Uptiq, an AI platform for financial services.

item.title: Genesis Global names James Harrison as CEO
item.description: Genesis Global today announced the appointment of James Harrison as CEO, succeeding his fellow co-founder Stephen Murphy, who is stepping down after ten successful years heading the firm.

item.title: EToro touts AI for social investing
item.description: eToro Group Ltd. (“eToro”, or the “Company”) (NASDAQ: ETOR), the trading and investing platform, announced today how the company is harnessing artificial intelligence to redefine social investing.

item.title: InvestCloud taps AI to drive advisor productivity
item.description: InvestCloud, a global leader in wealth technology, today announced the first of a new generation of AI-enabled solutions to help advisors increase productivity and deliver greater value to clients.

item.title: ERP platform Rillet raises $70m
item.description: Ambitious companies don’t rise or fall by product alone – they win or lose by how they run finance and accounting. Rillet, the AI-native ERP (enterprise resource planning) platform built for modern finance teams, today announced a $70 million Series B co-led by Andreessen Horowitz and ICONIQ with participation from Sequoia, Oak HC/FT and earlier investors.

item.title: EquiLend launches AI tool for instant market intelligence
item.description: EquiLend, a global fintech leader in securities finance, today introduced a new AI-powered assistant within its Data & Analytics suite, designed to help users interact with securities finance and short interest insights more efficiently.

item.title: Experian taps Sikoia for income and employment checks
item.description: Global data and technology company, Experian, and leading customer verification platform Sikoia today announce a new partnership to streamline and modernise income and employment verification.

item.title: VYRD and ZestyAI partner for home insurance analytics
item.description: VYRD, a Florida-based homeowners insurance company focused on delivering stability and protection in a challenging coastal market, has partnered with ZestyAI, the leading provider of AI-powered property risk analytics, to gain deeper visibility into property condition and risk exposure across its book of business.

item.title: Banco Sabadell picks DXC Technology for financial inclusion
item.description: DXC Technology (NYSE: DXC), a leading Fortune 500 global technology services provider, has been selected by Banco Sabadell, one of Spain’s leading banking groups, to help enhance the accessibility of its digital services.

item.title: HSLC picks Vine for AI-powered commercial lending
item.description: HSLC, $227 million community bank serving Ohio and Kentucky, will reduce commercial loan processing time from days to hours after implementing Vine’s AI-powered lending platform.

item.title: OLarry raises $10m for AI-powered tax planning
item.description: OLarry, an AI-powered tax firm for the next generation of high-net-worth individuals and businesses, today announced that it has raised a $10 million Series A round led by TTV Capital and joined by Walkabout Ventures and Marin Sonoma Impact Ventures, bringing the company's total funding to $14.5 million.

item.title: GoCardless rolls out AI tool for same-day direct debit settlement
item.description: Bank payment company GoCardless has announced the launch of a groundbreaking new feature to redefine same-day settlement for Direct Debit.

item.title: How to Handle AI Scepticism as a Banking Leader
item.description: Speaking to FinextraTV, Runki Goswami, Global Marketing Head & CMO at Newgen Software, and Varun Ghai, Associate Vice President (P&S), UK & EU, Newgen software discuss the use of AI and technology in banking against a backdrop of heightened customer needs. Explaining the different elements that are now available to banks, Goswami and Ghai also help to define what good leaders look like in this new era of technological change and scepticism.

item.title: The 3 Most Compelling AI Use Cases in Wealth Management
item.description: As part of our new Future of Wealth Series, Joe Bonanno, Global Head of Data Analytics & Innovation - Citi Wealth, Citi joined FinextraTV to discuss some of the key components of the current wealth management sector. Specifically, Bonanno brings insights into how AI is being receieved across the industry and the ways in which it is particularly useful in relation to data inputs for large language models, task optimisation and research.

item.title: How AI Can Reduce Decision Fatigue for Asset Managers
item.description: Joining the FinextraTV Virtual Studio, Phil Cox, COO, Russell Investments and Roshan Shetty, BFSI & Public Sector Head - Americas, Tech Mahindra discuss the decision fatigue within asset management currently and how AI helps alleviate that. Splitting the task abilities that can be improved into analysis, portfolio construction, risk management and trading strategies, Shetty and Cox debate the ease if AI adoption into existing processes and how information-based tools have risen to the top.

item.title: AI & Wealth: How to Build Trust Through Impact & Personalisation
item.description: Whilst at Money 20/20 in Amsterdam, Bahadir Yilmaz, Chief Analytics Officer, ING joined FinextraTV to provide some data insights in relation to growing AI conversations. Describing the challenge of taking data from its origins into actionable, presentable insights for clients, Yilmaz explains how a succesful, easy process will help to increase client's comfort with sharing larger, useful portions of data. Yilmaz, while speaking to AI use cases, says that clients are not interested in what happens behind-the-scenes, they care more for faster services and experiences where they can personally notice the change.

item.title: Australian Wealth: How Superannuation Growth is Prompting Technology Conversations
item.description: Whilst at the Communify Fincentric Experience 2025, Lachlan Hardy, Head of Sales & Business Development - APAC, Communify Fincentric helped to provide his unique insight into how the Australian wealth market compares to others. Speaking to the growth of self-directed wealth and superannuation funds, Hardy describes how one of the key considerations are around how technology is interacting and enabling this growth.

item.title: How A2A Innovation is Advancing The Fight Against Fraud
item.description: Speaking to FinextraTV in this interview, Helena Forest, EVP, Global Product & Commercial, Real Time Payments, Mastercard and Luke Reynolds, SVP, Financial Crimes, Mastercard discuss the impact of fraud and payment scams and look to address their prevention. As well as covering the many forms of fraud, such as APP fraud and romance scams, Forest and Reynolds explore the innovations currently available to help reduce its impact. They provide an honest optimism whilst remaining aware of the existing complexities within fraud prevention, bringing attention to the impact of the latest account to account fraud prevention methods including risk-scoring and Mastercard's UK A2A Sandbox.

item.title: How Partnerships Can Give Banks More Technological Agility
item.description: At the FinextraTV studio during Temenos Community Forum 2025, Monty Bhatia, EVP, Global Alliances and Partner Ecosystem, Temenos and Sudip Lahiri, Executive Vice President & Head - Europe & UKI, Financial Services, HCLTech spoke about core modernisation and AI implementation. Explaining the benefits of working with trusted partners, Lahiri and Bhatia explored how banks can effectively transition to an agile, modular architecture that enables fast implementation of AI-driven services.

item.title: How Rip-And-Replace Alternatives Help to Reimagine Banking
item.description: Sitting down with FinextraTV, Keith Redding, CRO, Universal Banking, Finastra discussed what it means to Reimagine Banking and why having the right technology is crucial in this new world. As well as providing real-world examples, Redding describes how the future is more about progressive transformation than 'rip and replace' and how this approach can help banks turn challenges into opportunities.

item.title: How to Drive Banking Innovation with Generative and Agentic AI
item.description: Whilst speaking at Temenos Community Forum 2025, Erik Johnson, Global Head of Product Design, Temenos joined the FinextraTV studio to talk about the areas he's most excited about within the development of AI. Discussing product ideation and compliance across both GenAI and Agentic AI, Johnson passionately positions his belief that AI is a tool that enables banks and partners to focus on more innovative and 'fun' day-to-day tasks. Beyond this, Johnson also describes the inherent connection between AI development and co-design.

item.title: What To Consider When Working with AI & Real-Time Data
item.description: In this interview at Money 20/20 2025 in Amsterdam, Rishi Doerga, Senior Solutions Engineer, Confluent helps to define real-time data and how it can be safely and correctly utilised alongside AI. When discussing best use cases for AI, Doerga advised a need to take a step back and consider how to manage vast amounts of data, as well as how to approach the data input when working with Agentic AI tools. Doerga also helps to provide some insights to those that require real-time data but are more apprehensive of AI.

item.title: How Younger Investors Will Change The Face of Wealth Management
item.description: Stepping away from the Temenos Community Forum 2025, Eric Mellor, Wealth Management Specialist, MEA and APAC, Temenos joined the FinextraTV studio to talk about the massive shift of wealth towards younger generations. Explaining the challenges that will occur as this trend continues, Mellor emphasises how quickly those in the industry will lose customers if they don't prepare now. Describing how younger investors are more interested in ESG, digital experiences and concierge services, Mellor highlights the evolution of wealth management in this new era and the technology that can help financial institutions adjust.

item.title: How Technology Can Enable an Overdue Rethink of Corporate Lending
item.description: At the Temenos Community Forum 2025, Maurya Murphy, Senior Product Director (Corporate), Temenos and Mohammad Khatib, Director of Information Technology, The Arab Energy Fund, joined FinextraTV's studio to discuss the challenges within corporate lending technology. Investigating where legacy systems need modernising, Murphy and Khatib outline emerging technologies that enable an easier, more corporate-friendly approach to loan lifecycles.

item.title: What Will The Next Generation of the Investing Landscape Look Like?
item.description: Whilst attending the Communify Fincentric Experience 2025, Brian Casey, CEO, Westwood Holding Group, joined the FinextraTV studio and shared his thoughts on the event, the importance of networking and how exciting the next generation of investing will be. As part of this next generation, Casey says investors will continue to look for objective advice and guidance against the backdrop of a plethora of options.

item.title: Modernising for what’s next: Data-led innovation in financial services
item.description: AI is here – but is the industry’s data ready?

item.title: What’s next in wealth? Exploring European trends & priorities
item.description: How can wealth managers best serve the next generation of investors?

item.title: Growing pains: Evolving core banking for an age of AI
item.description: From aging foundations to digital-first banking and AI.

item.title: How smart FIs are achieving their AI transformation targets
item.description: Discover the most successful AI use cases in banking.

item.title: What Are Liquid Neural Networks? The Next Big Leap in Adaptive AI
item.description: Liquid Neural Networks: The Next Step in Making AI That Thinks Like a Brain What if AI could keep l...

item.title: What Is Vibe Coding? A Beginner’s Guide to the Coding Movement You Can’t Ignore
item.description: What does it mean to "vibe code"? Why Gen Z Is Changing the Way We Program Programming use...

item.title: Original rambling story telling here ...ChatGDP shortened and improved it in the previous post
item.description: AI-impact: 20-30% GDP growth a year or no more than 1-2% over 10 years? Quite a difference between t...

item.title: AI Is a Tool, Not the Vision — Don’t Let It Decide What You Build
item.description: AI technology has seen a meteoric rise in the past several years, and by now it’s far from being a n...

item.title: What could go wrong when fintech over-rely on AI, and how to fix it
item.description: Financial services is one of the best-positioned industries to leverage all the benefits AI has to o...

item.title: Sponsor's message: [New Report] Modern Payments Excellence: Exploring Diversification of Channels, Orchestration, and Regulation
item.description: [New Report] Modern Payments Excellence: Exploring Diversification of Channels, Orchestration, and Regulation
